headline: Exclusive-Apple supplier Tata tightens internal controls after data breach, sources say
summary: Tata Electronics, a key Indian supplier to Apple, has restricted internal access to sensitive systems as it investigates a leak of thousands of secret client files on the dark web, a Tata source and two industry officials said.  Tata has ‌also hired a global consultant to conduct a forensic audit and has reported the incident to the Indian government and its clients, said the Tata ‌source, declining to be named given the sensitivity of the matter.  Reuters reported this week that ransomware group World Leaks posted more than 200,000 files to the dark web, including purported component design papers from Apple and ​Tesla, both of which are Tata clients.

content.title: Exclusive-Apple supplier Tata tightens internal controls after data breach, sources say
content.summary: Tata Electronics, a key Indian supplier to Apple, has restricted internal access to sensitive systems as it investigates a leak of thousands of secret client files on the dark web, a Tata source and two industry officials said.  Tata has ‌also hired a global consultant to conduct a forensic audit and has reported the incident to the Indian government and its clients, said the Tata ‌source, declining to be named given the sensitivity of the matter.  Reuters reported this week that ransomware group World Leaks posted more than 200,000 files to the dark web, including purported component design papers from Apple and ​Tesla, both of which are Tata clients.

headline: The Week in Numbers: Micron climbs high, Apple raises prices
summary:
STORY: From Micron's mega market cap to Apple's price hikes...

...this is the Week in Numbers.

:: The Week in Numbers

:: $1.4 trillion.

About $1.4 trillion was Micron's market cap after it briefly blew past the value of Meta and Tesla for the first time.

It came after the firm forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations.

Micron is a major memory-chip maker and a key supplier to AI titan Nvidia.

The company's shares have more than tripled in value so far this year, and soared close to a fifth following its results.

:: 5%

Almost 5% is how far Apple shares fell after it announced price hikes.

The tech giant said its cheapest laptop will jump from $599 to $699, with iPad prices also rising - although the iPhone won't jump for now.

Apple says it can no longer avoid passing on rising costs to consumers.

The price of memory chips jumped by around 98% in the first quarter, and has kept soaring due to the AI boom.

:: 42% 

42% was how much shares of Wendy's soared as retail traders bought up the beaten-down stock in the latest meme-like rally.

Trading on the heavily shorted stock was paused multiple times due to volatility..

Shares briefly hit a more than seven-month high.

The fast food chain is battling weak sales and pressure from an activist investor, with its stock down more than 78% from record highs five years ago.

:: $648 billion

Almost $648 billion is how much Samsung plans to invest in South Korea over 10 years.

That's according to the Maeil Business Newspaper, which said the firm will announce its intentions on Monday.

The investment includes a potential $194 billion to build chip factories in the country's southwest, as well as plans for AI data centers, batteries and displays.

There was no immediate response to a request for comment from Samsung.

:: $606.5 million

And $606.5 million was H&M's operating profit during its fiscal second quarter.

That was smaller than expected for the Swedish fashion retailer.

H&M blamed weaker consumer demand and inflation made worse by the Iran war.

CEO Daniel Erver said Europe, particularly the western part of it, had gone through a difficult quarter.
